ALL SPECIALTIES / PHARMACY TECHNICIAN / ARIZONA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Arizona Pharmacy Technician License

Arizona Pharmacy Technician license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-18 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Pharmacy Technician applications in Arizona run through Arizona Board of Pharmacy. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official Transcripts - Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score Report - Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 2 to 4 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Arizona by submitting a new application to the Arizona Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Arizona-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 2 to 4 Weeks.

  1. STEP 1

    Review Eligibility & Gather Documents

    Visit the Arizona State Board of Pharmacy's Pharmacy Technician page (https://pharmacy.az.gov/new-applicants/pharmacy-technician) to review full requirements. Gather necessary documents including a copy of your government-issued photo ID, high school diploma or GED equivalent, and the completed Pharmacy Technician Attestation Form (available at https://pharmacy.az.gov/forms).

    Fee: $82 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Initiate Fingerprint Background Check

    Complete the fingerprint background check process as detailed on the Board's Fingerprint Background Checks page (https://pharmacy.az.gov/fingerprint-background-checks). This typically involves obtaining a fingerprint card and submitting it to the Board's designated vendor according to their instructions.

    Fee: $82 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Create Online Account

    Navigate to the Arizona Board of Pharmacy Licensing Portal (https://azph.az.gov/licensure/) and click the "create one" link under the "New Account" section to establish your applicant profile with the Board.

    Fee: $82 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Complete & Submit Application

    Log in to the Arizona Board of Pharmacy Licensing Portal using your new account. Select "New Application" for Pharmacy Technician and meticulously complete all required sections. Upload your photo ID, diploma/GED, and Pharmacy Technician Attestation Form directly within the online application.

    Fee: $82 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Pay Application Fee

    Electronically submit the required application fee through the Arizona Board of Pharmacy Licensing Portal to finalize your application submission. Ensure all sections are complete and documents uploaded before payment.

    Fee: $82 · Timeline: 2 to 4 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$82
Background check
Fingerprint clearance card fee paid separately
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$82 plus fingerprint clearance card costs

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Board Contact

Board
Arizona Board of Pharmacy
Phone
(602) 771-2727
Website
pharmacy.az.gov/technician-license-information
Application page
Open application

Renewal

Pharmacy Technician renewal in Arizona follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$72
Continuing education
20 hours per renewal cycle

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Arizona pharmacy technician license cost?

The Arizona State Board of Pharmacy lists an $82 pharmacy technician application fee and a $72 renewal fee.

Is national certification required?

Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

Does Arizona have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Arizona. To work in Arizona, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Arizona?

The Arizona State Board of Pharmacy does not publish a public pharmacy technician processing timeline on its licensing page. Use the board's application status tools to check progress.

Can I start working in Arizona while my endorsement application is processing?

Contact the Arizona Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Arizona.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$82

Processing time

2 to 4 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

See board

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 2 years · $72

Licensing board

Arizona Board of Pharmacy

pharmacy.az.gov/technician-license-information
